# OhMyDoc — QA Regression Suite

Playwright end-to-end tests for [OhMyDoc](https://ohmydoc.vercel.app), an AI-powered document formatter.

## What's tested

| Group | Scenarios |
|-------|-----------|
| **1. Basic Load** | Page title, textarea visible, Format button disabled when empty, placeholder text, no console errors, button enables with sufficient input |
| **2. Happy Path — Resume** | Full resume formatting flow, loading state, preview, no raw XML tags, content preservation, HTML structure in output |
| **3. Happy Path — Cover Letter** | Cover letter formatting, content verification |
| **4. Mutation Warning** | DOM inspection for divergence/mutation warning elements, formatted content assertion |
| **5. PDF Export** | Export button disabled before formatting, enabled after, `window.print()` triggered |
| **6. Validation** | Empty and short input keep Format button disabled |
| **7. Skeleton Loading** | Shimmer appears during load, disappears on completion |
| **8. Sample Content** | Sample content availability (button or placeholder), formatting of sample-like content |

**Total: 16 tests** across 8 feature areas (test 8b skips automatically when no dedicated sample button is present).

## Requirements

- Node.js 18+
- Chromium (installed automatically by Playwright)

## Setup

```bash
npm install
npx playwright install chromium
```

## Running tests

```bash
# Run all tests
npm test

# Run with visible browser
npx playwright test --headed

# Run a specific test group
npx playwright test --grep "8"

# Open HTML report after a run
npm run test:report
```

## Configuration

Tests run against `https://ohmydoc.vercel.app` by default.

Override with the `BASE_URL` environment variable:

```bash
BASE_URL=https://staging.ohmydoc.vercel.app npx playwright test
```

Or create a `.env` file:

```
BASE_URL=https://ohmydoc.vercel.app
```

## CI notes

- GitHub Actions workflow at `.github/workflows/ci.yml` — runs on push, PR, and weekly schedule
- Tests run serially (1 worker) — the AI formatting API must not be rate-limited by parallel calls
- AI-dependent tests use a 90s timeout with fast-fail on API errors
- `test.slow()` is set on tests that involve AI calls (expected ~10–20s each)
- Screenshots are saved on failure under `test-results/`
- HTML report is saved as a CI artifact under `playwright-report/`
- Override the target URL via the `BASE_URL` repository variable in GitHub Actions

## Known observations

- **No "Load Sample" button**: Sample content is shown as textarea placeholder text only. Test 8b skips cleanly; tests 8a/8c adapt accordingly. A dedicated "Try Sample" button would improve first-visit UX.
- **Mutation warning**: A divergence banner appears after formatting (observed in test 4) — this is expected behavior when AI rewrites content significantly (>5% divergence).
- **Error phrase detection**: `waitForFormattingOutcome` only fast-fails on API key configuration phrases, not generic words like "error" or "failed", to avoid false positives on normal UI text.

name: Playwright Tests

on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
  pull_request:
    branches: [main]
  schedule:
    # Weekly smoke run every Monday at 08:00 UTC
    - cron: '0 8 * * 1'

jobs:
  test:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    timeout-minutes: 30

    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: 20
          cache: npm

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: npm ci

      - name: Install Chromium
        run: npx playwright install chromium --with-deps

      - name: Run Playwright tests
        run: npx playwright test
        env:
          BASE_URL: ${{ vars.BASE_URL || 'https://ohmydoc.vercel.app' }}

      - name: Upload HTML report
        if: always()
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: playwright-report
          path: playwright-report/
          retention-days: 30

      - name: Upload JSON results
        if: always()
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: test-results-json
          path: test-reports/results.json
          retention-days: 30

I worked closely with engineering and product teams using Figma, conducting usability testing with 12+ participants per sprint cycle. My work earned an internal Design Excellence Award in 2024.

I admire Acme's recent launch of the accessibility-first design toolkit and would love to contribute to this work. My background in inclusive design and WCAG compliance aligns closely with your team's values.

Thank you for your time and consideration. I would welcome the chance to discuss how I can contribute to Acme's product vision. Please find my portfolio attached.

Sincerely,
Jane Park

All 9 pass against the local dev server. ## Key Decisions - Dev server started on port **3002** (not 3000 as noted in task; port was taken), so default BASE_URL in the spec is `http://localhost:3002` — overridable via `BASE_URL` env var. - Test 2's `getByText('Try a sample:')` matched two elements (action bar `` and empty-state `

`). Fixed by scoping to `span.sample-label` to resolve strict-mode violation. - Test 5 verifies the `.sample-link` action bar buttons (always in DOM) rather than the empty-state UButtons, which disappear from view when the skeleton overlay takes over during formatting. - Used `expect(async () => ...).toPass()` pattern for textarea value checks after async sample load, matching the Vue reactivity timing pattern in the existing test files. ## PR / Branch - Committed directly to `main` per task instructions (no PR).
